data.title: NVIDIA introduces 5-part plan for enterprise RAG systems to tap into $10.5B market by 2030.
data.description: NVIDIA has introduced the Enterprise RAG Blueprint, which offers a modular architecture for multimodal AI knowledge systems. This blueprint is aimed at capturing a significant share of the projected $10.5 billion RAG tooling market by 2030. The goal is to provide a versatile and efficient solution for businesses looking to implement AI technologies.

data.title: Pagos expands MCP Server for merchants to access verified payments data via AI chat agents.
data.description: In Los Angeles on February 17, 2026, Pagos, a company specializing in AI-driven payments intelligence, unveiled a significant enhancement to its Model Context Protocol (MCP) Server. This update allows merchants to now retrieve their authenticated and unified payments information via AI chat agents.

data.title: Ormco Forum 2026 unites top orthodontic leaders globally, showcasing advances in orthodontics. #BREA #PRNewswire #OrthodonticsGoodAtOrmco™
data.description: Ormco™ Corporation, a top player in orthodontic products and education for over six decades, is hosting The Ormco Forum 2026, an event highlighting cutting-edge advancements in orthodontics. Set to take place in BREA, Calif., on February 17, 2026, this premier clinical education event will draw attention to new approaches shaping the field.

data.title: Cacique Intimates releases Lightest Lift™ bra with 40% lighter padding for cups up to F, G and H. Clever design aids gravity-defying lift.
data.description: Cacique Intimates, a popular fuller cup bra brand based in Columbus, Ohio, launched Lightest Lift, a new bra with padding that is 40% lighter than the average bra. The Lightest Lift bra offers airy support and impressive lift, even in cup sizes up to F, G, and H. This innovative design provides a gravity-defying experience for customers seeking comfort and style in their undergarments. The lightweight padding sets this bra apart as a groundbreaking solution in the lingerie industry.

data.title: Expert Jason Guck discusses small business energy risks and benefits of microgrids in Rochester, NY. When to consider switch?
data.description: In Rochester, New York on February 17, 2026, an article discusses the advantages of microgrids in enhancing reliability for small and medium-sized businesses (SMBs). It explores the factors businesses should consider when deciding to opt for a microgrid instead of solely depending on the local utility. This piece emphasizes the importance of weighing the costs associated with implementing a microgrid against the risks of experiencing outages and the potential daily savings. HelloNation offers insights on this topic, aiming to aid SMBs in making informed decisions.

data.title: Vibe aims to target tech insiders with specialized billboards in the Bay Area, setting new standards in microtargeting.
data.description: There has been a rise in billboards in the Bay Area that may seem perplexing to those not in the tech industry. Notably, a company has elevated the practice of microtargeting to a new high.

data.title: Pender Growth Fund to undertake Normal Course Issuer Bid on TSXV, subject to acceptance.
data.description: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on February 17, 2026, Pender Growth Fund Inc. (TSXV: PTF) declared its intention to initiate a Normal Course Issuer Bid (NCIB) to the TSX Venture Exchange (TSXV). The company plans to repurchase a certain number of its outstanding common shares through the TSXV. The bid is pending approval from the TSXV.

data.title: Dragonfly secures $650M fund, spotlighting real-world assets as crypto VC landscape evolves towards core financial infrastructure.
data.description: The recent increase in funding for Dragonfly demonstrates a shift in crypto venture capital towards tokenized real-world assets, payments, and core financial infrastructure crucial for institutional involvement. This highlights the growing importance of these sectors in the crypto market.

data.title: Inventor from Katy, Texas develops C C WAND for cleaning (HOF-815), reducing physical strain and efficiency in cleaning.
data.description: In Pittsburgh on February 17, 2026, an inventor from Katy, Texas shared the inspiration behind the creation of the C C WAND. According to the inventor, the aim was to develop a more efficient method for cleaning small, narrow, and inaccessible spaces within households. The innovative design of the C C WAND not only simplifies the cleaning process in such areas, but also diminishes physical strain associated with conventional cleaning methods. By introducing this product, the inventor eliminated the need to exert excessive effort while cleaning tight spaces, revolutionizing the cleaning experience for users.

data.title: CDN market to hit USD 40,161M by 2032 due to rise in OTT, cloud, and edge computing, says Credence Research.
data.description: On February 17, 2026, a report titled "Content Delivery Network (CDN) Market – Growth, Share, Opportunities & Competitive Analysis, 2024 – 2032" was incorporated into the offerings of Credence Research Inc. The worldwide Content Delivery Network (CDN) Market is projected to witness significant growth from the current year through 2032.

data.title: 2 out of 3 proxy advisory firms recommend shareholders to vote against Jack in the Box Chairman David Goebel due to poor performance.
data.description: Glass Lewis recently released a report criticizing David Goebel's performance, calling it "exceptionally poor" and highlighting a lack of tangible accountability. They recommended against his election, emphasizing the need for urgent board-level changes. Egan-Jones also advised against Goebel's election, echoing Glass Lewis' concerns and emphasizing the necessity for immediate board level reforms. Both analyses indicate a strong consensus for significant changes and accountability in the company's leadership.
